---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-worldmap3.png')
background-size: 105%
background-class: top

.center[.size50[**IV. What is the Future of Transnational Politics and IR?**]]

<br>

.size50[
**Today's Agenda**

- Predicting the Future: Threats to the United States of America
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. EVAL Day!

2. Review cases submitted to Canvas

<br>



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[
.content-box-white[**IDEA Course Evaluations**]

<br>

.content-box-white[https://drury.campuslabs.com/eval-home/]
]

???

<br>

<br>

Today I'd like us to consider how the role of the United States in the world is likely to change over the next 25 years.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size65[.content-box-purple[**For Today**]]

.size50[
What is the single biggest international threat facing the United States over the next 25 years? 

- Submit APA evidence and explanation to Canvas

- Be specific, try to avoid overlap!
]

???

For today I asked each of you to bring evidence to class that answers this question!

### Everybody ready to go?

<br>

Everybody take a few minutes to review the options submitted to Canvas.

- Your job is to identify the **FIVE** biggest threats from the options

<br>

### Questions?

- Go!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-Red-Brushes.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center, inverse

.size70[**What is the single biggest international threat facing the United States over the next 25 years? **]

???

### Ok, let's see where you ended up. Somebody give us their top 5!

- *ON BOARD*

- *Go around the room adding to the list with new options or add a tally next to repeated options*

<br>

*Once you have enough to make four groups*

Alright, here are our top-four options

- *Split class into groups by option*

- Go sit with your group!

<br>

GROUPS, you have 10 mins to build an argument diagram ON THE BOARD!

- Premises to the conclusion!

<br>

### Questions?

- Go!

<br>

*PRESENT and DISCUSS each*

<br>

### Where do we end up? Why?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-Red-Brushes.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center, inverse

.size70[**What single thing should the US do to prepare for the threats we face over the next 25 years?**]

???

Given this list of threats, what should we do about it?

- Talk to your group and get ready to report back!

<br>

*PRESENT and DISCUSS each*






---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size65[.content-box-white[**Assignment for Next Class**]]

<br>

.size50[
Get ready for Paper 3!

- Collect and review your notes for all of the IR models we have explored this term
]

???

### Questions on the assignment?

<br>

- Neorealism
- Offensive Realism
- Liberal Peace
- Economic Liberalism
- Bargaining Model of War
- Prisoner's Dilemma
- Two-Level Bargaining
- Constructivism
- Feminist Theory
- Postcolonial Theory
